notes.anchor: the Fourth and the Martian legions
notes.note: Two veteran legions that deserted Antony for the Republican cause in late 44 BC; their reassignment away from Decimus Brutus left him, as he complains here, with only raw recruits.
The townsfolk of Vicetia (modern Vicenza) in Cisalpine Gaul; Decimus Brutus asks Cicero to shield them from a hostile motion in the Senate concerning their home-born slaves.
Vernae, slaves born within the household rather than purchased; a status dispute over such slaves at Vicetia underlies the Senate business Decimus Brutus references.

notes.anchor: The twelfth day before the Kalends of June, at Vercellae.
notes.note: Dated by inclusive Roman counting back from the Kalends (first) of June, i.e. 21 May 43 BC; Vercellae (modern Vercelli) lay in the upper Po valley of Cisalpine Gaul.
